Article.ArticleTitle: Preoperative Multiparametric MRI-Based Tumour-Periprostatic Adipose Tissue Interface Characterisation for Extraprostatic Extension Prediction in Prostate Cancer.

Abstract.AbstractText: To evaluate the independent predictive value of tumour-periprostatic adipose tissue (PPAT) interface features on preoperative multiparametric magnetic resonance imaging (mpMRI) for extraprostatic extension (EPE) in prostate cancer and to compare discrimination and clinical net benefit with a baseline clinical model.

This single-centre retrospective cohort included patients who underwent radical prostatectomy with mpMRI completed within 8 weeks. On a single axial slice at maximum tumour diameter, five simplified interface features were measured using standard PACS tools: contact length, contact angle, T2 signal intensity ratio, interface apparent diffusion coefficient (3-mm annular zone) and capsular integrity score (0-2 scale). A baseline clinical model (prostate-specific antigen [PSA], PSA density, PI-RADS and biopsy Gleason score) and a combined model (baseline variables plus LASSO-selected interface features) were constructed. Bootstrap internal validation (1000 iterations) with bias correction was performed. Discrimination was assessed using the area under the curve (AUC), and calibration curves and decision curve analysis evaluated accuracy and net clinical benefit.

A total of 240 patients were included, with an EPE prevalence of 34.2% (82/240). The combined model achieved a bias-corrected AUC of 0.823 (95% confidence interval [CI]: 0.768-0.878), suggesting improvement over the baseline model's AUC of 0.744 (95% CI: 0.680-0.808). Decision curve analysis revealed a higher net benefit for the combined model across clinically relevant threshold probabilities (10%-50%).
Simplified tumour-PPAT interface features independently predict EPE without increasing imaging complexity, improving discrimination and clinical value for preoperative risk stratification.

Reference.Citation: Mehralivand S., Shih J. H., Harmon S., et al., “A Grading System for the Assessment of Risk of Extraprostatic Extension of Prostate Cancer at Multiparametric MRI,” Radiology 290, no. 3 (2019): 709–719, 10.1148/radiol.2018181278.

Kim T. H., Woo S., Han S., et al., “The Diagnostic Performance of the Length of Tumor Capsular Contact on MRI for Detecting Prostate Cancer Extraprostatic Extension: A Systematic Review and Meta‐Analysis,” Korean Journal of Radiology 21, no. 6 (2020): 684–694, 10.3348/kjr.2019.0842.

Drewa J., Lazar‐Juszczak K., Adamowicz J., and Juszczak K., “Periprostatic Adipose Tissue as a Contributor to Prostate Cancer Pathogenesis: A Narrative Review,” Cancers 17, no. 3 (2025): 372, 10.3390/cancers17030372.

Xu L., Zhang G., Zhao L., et al., “Radiomics Based on Multiparametric Magnetic Resonance Imaging to Predict Extraprostatic Extension of Prostate Cancer,” Frontiers in Oncology 10 (2020): 940, 10.3389/fonc.2020.00940.

Cuocolo R., Stanzione A., Faletti R., et al., “MRI Index Lesion Radiomics and Machine Learning for Detection of Extraprostatic Extension of Disease: A Multicenter Study,” European Radiology 31, no. 10 (2021): 7575–7583, 10.1007/s00330-021-07856-3.

Zwanenburg A., Vallières M., Abdalah M. A., et al., “The Image Biomarker Standardization Initiative: Standardized Quantitative Radiomics for High‐Throughput Image‐Based Phenotyping,” Radiology 295, no. 2 (2020): 328–338, 10.1148/radiol.2020191145.

Guerra A., Wang H., Orton M. R., et al., “Prediction of Extracapsular Extension of Prostate Cancer by MRI Radiomic Signature: A Systematic Review,” Insights Into Imaging 15, no. 1 (2024): 217, 10.1186/s13244-024-01776-8.

Lazzereschi L., Birks J., and Colling R., “Does the Extent of Extraprostatic Extension at Radical Prostatectomy Predict Outcome?‐A Systematic Review and Meta‐Analysis,” Histopathology 85, no. 5 (2024): 727–742, 10.1111/his.15292.

Sato S., Takahashi H., Kimura T., Egawa S., Furusato B., and Ikegami M., “Clinicopathological Importance of Anterior Prostate Cancer in Japanese Men,” Pathology International 67, no. 3 (2017): 156–162, 10.1111/pin.12513.

Martini A., Wever L., Soeterik T. F. W., et al., “An Updated Model for Predicting Side‐Specific Extraprostatic Extension in the Era of MRI‐Targeted Biopsy,” Prostate Cancer and Prostatic Diseases 27, no. 3 (2024): 520–524, 10.1038/s41391-023-00776-x.

Di Trani M. G., Galea N., Rossi Espagnet M. C., et al., “Performance of Diffusion Kurtosis Imaging Versus Diffusion Tensor Imaging in Discriminating Between Benign Tissue, Low‐ and High‐Gleason Grade Prostate Cancer,” Academic Radiology 26, no. 10 (2019): 1328–1337, 10.1016/j.acra.2018.11.015.
